mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is an enterprise Kubernetes distribution that provides a developer-friendly platform for deploying and managing containerized applications. It includes additional features such as a developer console, integrated CI/CD, and enhanced security. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for a subscription for enterprise features.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI) tool, which is essential for interacting with your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).tar.gz

tar -xvf oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).tar.gz

sudo mv oc /usr/local/bin/

oc version

```

> Download the latest OpenShift CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH
> Verify the installation of the OpenShift CLI

steps.title: Create an OpenShift Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new OpenShift cluster using the OpenShift Installer. You will specify the version, node count, and region.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=<INSTALL_DIR> --log-level=info

```

> Replace <INSTALL_DIR> with your desired installation directory

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow access to your new OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=<INSTALL_DIR>/auth/kubeconfig

oc login -u kubeadmin -p <PASSWORD> https://<CLUSTER_API_URL>:6443

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable
> Replace <PASSWORD> and <CLUSTER_API_URL> with your cluster's details

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes setting up monitoring, autoscaling, and RBAC for your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-config.yaml

oc adm policy add-cluster-role-to-user cluster-admin <USERNAME>

```

> Apply the monitoring configuration
> Replace <USERNAME> with the user you want to grant admin privileges

uninstall.title: Uninstall OpenShift Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=<INSTALL_DIR> --log-level=info

```

> Replace <INSTALL_DIR> with your installation directory

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers, PVCs, and DNS records are removed.

```bash

kubectl delete pvc --all

kubectl delete services --all

```

> Delete all persistent volume claims
> Delete all services

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenShift Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster data. This step will guide you through the upgrade process.

```bash

oc adm upgrade --to=<NEW_VERSION>

oc get nodes

oc get clusterversion

```

> Replace <NEW_VERSION> with the target version
> Verify that all nodes are ready after the upgrade
> Check the cluster version to confirm the upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'oc login: error: unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The OpenShift API server may be down or unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the OpenShift API server and network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more nodes may not be in a ready state due to resource constraints or configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Inspect the node status and logs using 'oc describe node <NODE_NAME>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The image may not be available in the specified registry.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and check the image registry for availability.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified resource may not exist in the current namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are in the correct namespace or check the resource name.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced enhanced security features, improved developer experience with new CLI commands.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the new security features and update your authentication methods accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'oc get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and sufficient resources for the cluster.
